homepage.title: Laurence Turner - working hard for Northfield
homepage.text_excerpt: Laurence Turner - working hard for Northfield WORKING HARD FOR NORTHFIELD Proud to be Labour MP for Allens Cross, Great Park, Kings Norton, Longbridge, New Frankley, Rednal, Rubery, Shenley, Weoley Castle, Weoley Hill, and West Heath LAURENCE TURNER Home Latest news and views Contact and support Check if you are a constituent How to get in touch See what I can help with Office standards About me Get updates Donate Working hard to represent you Welcome It is an honour to be elected as Birmingham Northfield’s Member of Parliament. I will work hard every day to repay the faith that has been placed in me. You can use this website to find out about my work in Parliament and find contact details for casework support. You can also subscribe to a regular constituency newsletter . Key links Find constituency office details and get in contact GET IN TOUCH See if I can assist with casework and other issues GET SUPPORT Get regular local and national updates NEWSLETTER Record since July 2024 0 + constituent cases closed 0 + emails sent 0 times spoken in Parliament 0 written questions asked Latest news Why SEND needs reform This article appeared in the Birmingham Mail on 23 February. pages.title: How to get in touch – Laurence Turner - working hard for Northfield
pages.text_excerpt: How to get in touch – Laurence Turner - working hard for Northfield LAURENCE TURNER Home Latest news and views Contact and support Check if you are a constituent How to get in touch See what I can help with Office standards About me Get updates Donate How to get in touch You can get in touch using the form opposite. You can also contact me in the following ways: Email laurence.turner.mp@parliament.uk Phone 0121 516 5770 Constituency office (note - meetings are by appointment only). The Factory 5 Devon Way Longbridge B31 2TS Please include your home address in written correspondence so that I can prioritise messages from constituents. Please note also that surgeries are not held at the office - they are held at sites across the constituency on a rotating basis. Full Name Email address First line of your address Postcode Subscribe to a regular e-newsletter? Message Send message Promoted by and on behalf of Laurence Turner of Terry Duffy House, 1 Thomas Street, B70 6NT Privacy policy Acceptable use policy Cookie Preferences This website was built at no cost to taxpayers with Quarto

pages.title: See what I can help with – Laurence Turner - working hard for Northfield
pages.h1: See what I can help with
headings: h2: What I can help with
pages.text_excerpt: See what I can help with – Laurence Turner - working hard for Northfield LAURENCE TURNER Home Latest news and views Contact and support Check if you are a constituent How to get in touch See what I can help with Office standards About me Get updates Donate See what I can help with If you are a constituent in Birmingham Northfield and you are experiencing difficulties then please contact me if you think I can assist. I am here to help and represent you, and I will assist if I can. Please note that Parliament operates under a strict code which means that I can only take up issues on behalf of people who live in the constituency. You can click here to check if I am your MP. What I can help with MPs can help to make representations to public bodies on your behalf when you aren’t getting the right treatment, such as: DWP HMRC The Home Office Birmingham City Council Please include any reference numbers that you hold, and copies of correspondence from those bodies if you have them. Councillors are also elected to hold Birmingham City Council to account and they should be contacted in the first instance on council matters. You can find and contact your councillors here . I might also be able to raise policy issues in Parliament. You can read more here . Surgeries I hold regular surgeries across the constituency. Please contact me and include your postal address and a summary of the issue to request an appointment. A member of the team will be in touch. Many problems can be resolved faster over the phone or by email and my staff may make suggestions on the best way of taking a matter forward. Sources of local support My team can suggest local sources of support, but you may wish to contact those organisations directly: Birmingham City Council : Cost of living support https://www.birmingham.gov.uk/livingsupport Northfield Community Partnership offers high-quality advice and assistance with a wide range of issues, including social housing and benefits matters (not including PIP), and food parcels. 0121 411 2157 - https://www.northfieldcommunity.org/ Birmingham Citizens Advice Bureau can assist with a number of matters, including PIP applications: 0808 278 7990 - https://www.bcabs.org.uk/ Birmingham SENDIASS free advice on special educational needs and disabilities (SEND), arms-length from Birmingham City Council: 0121 303 5004 - https://www.birminghamsendiass.co.uk/ St Bart’s Pantry food pantry in Allen’s Cross: 07568 528 826 - https://www.yourlocalpantry.co.uk/pantry-listings/birmingham-st-bart-s-pantry/ B30 & South Birmingham Foodbank in Kings Norton: 07582 143 972 - https://b30.foodbank.org.uk/ FoodCycle Weoley Castle offers a free weekly hot meal at St Gabriels Church: https://foodcycle.org.uk/location/foodcycle-birmingham-weoley-castle/ NewStarts in Frankley can offer free furniture, household supplies, and food support to those who need it: 0121 679 2072 - https://newstarts.org.uk/ Visiting Parliament I can help with arranging visits to Parliament school visits and getting tickets for Prime Minister’s Questions - although please note that the dates for school visits and PMQs book up months in advance. Promoted by and on behalf of Laurence Turner of Terry Duffy House, 1 Thomas Street, B70 6NT Privacy policy Acceptable use policy Cookie Preferences This website was built at no cost to taxpayers with Quarto

pages.title: About me – Laurence Turner - working hard for Northfield
pages.text_excerpt: About me – Laurence Turner - working hard for Northfield LAURENCE TURNER Home Latest news and views Contact and support Check if you are a constituent How to get in touch See what I can help with Office standards About me Get updates Donate About me I stood to be your MP to deliver better public services and better protections at work. I know what it takes to represent different communities. Before I was elected as MP in July 2024, I worked as head of policy at a trade union – the GMB - where I fought for better for working people every day. I changed the law to double sentences for abusers who sexually assault police officers, ambulance workers and firefighters. I campaigned for and delivered better pay for thousands of key workers. For me, making life better for working people is what the Labour Party should be about. I was elected with a mandate to fight for better public services. Those services were there for me when I needed them. I was a SEND kid who went on to become a senior trade unionist and ultimately run for Parliament. That’s why I am passionate about reforming the SEND system that is failing too many children and families. I know what it’s like to be a victim of serious violent crime and I will always stand for safer streets across our community. Birmingham needs MPs who will stand up for the city in Parliament. Most of my family are from south Birmingham and I live in Northfield. I will always fight for investment and opportunity in our communities. Since the election, I’ve fufilled my promises to restore an advertised constituency office, hold regular surgeries, and be a locally-based MP. All my resources are here, in Birmingham. I am one of the most active MPs in Parliament where I regularly raise constituency issues, and I scrutinise Government policy as a member of the Transport Select Committee. In Parliament, I chair the GMB trade union group of MPs and Labour’s backbench business group on Climate Change and Net Zero. I am also an officer of the following All Party Parliamentary Groups: West Midlands City Regions Transport Sustainable Plastics Belarus Archives and History I will always speak up for Birmingham Northfield in Parliament and fight for working people and the better public services that we need. Promoted by and on behalf of Laurence Turner of Terry Duffy House, 1 Thomas Street, B70 6NT Privacy policy Acceptable use policy Cookie Preferences This website was built at no cost to taxpayers with Quarto

pages.text_excerpt: Petition: fix the potholes on Rednal Road – Laurence Turner - working hard for Northfield LAURENCE TURNER Home Latest news and views Contact and support Check if you are a constituent How to get in touch See what I can help with Office standards About me Get updates Donate Petition: fix the potholes on Rednal Road Public services Author Laurence Published February 17, 2026 Sign our petition to fix Rednal Road. If you are not redirected, click here . The potholes on Rednal Road are a menace. Please sign this petition, organised by Carmel Corrigan (Councillor for Kings Norton North) and Laurence Turner (MP for Birmingham Northfield), which will be presented to Birmingham City Council for action.
